tossups.question: This collection contains a story in which Kala Nag takes a boy named Little Toomai into the forest for “the dance of the elephants.” In another story from this collection, Kaa hypnotizes the Bandar-log monkeys. A third story from this collection features an English family who is saved from the cobras Nag and Nagaina by a friendly mongoose. This collection includes (*) “Tiger! Tiger” and “Rikki-Tikki-Tavi,” as well as a story in which a child is raised by wolves and uses fire to drive off the murderous tiger Shere Khan. (sheer khan). For 10 points, name this collection by Rudyard Kipling that features the “man-cub” Mowgli and his companions Bagheera and Baloo.
tossups.question_sanitized: This collection contains a story in which Kala Nag takes a boy named Little Toomai into the forest for "the dance of the elephants." In another story from this collection, Kaa hypnotizes the Bandar-log monkeys. A third story from this collection features an English family who is saved from the cobras Nag and Nagaina by a friendly mongoose. This collection includes (*) "Tiger! Tiger" and "Rikki-Tikki-Tavi," as well as a story in which a child is raised by wolves and uses fire to drive off the murderous tiger Shere Khan. (sheer khan). For 10 points, name this collection by Rudyard Kipling that features the "man-cub" Mowgli and his companions Bagheera and Baloo.

tossups.question: Karim is asked by Shadwell to wear a loincloth and use an accent while performing in a play of this name in The Buddha of Suburbia. A collection of adult satirical stories with this name was written by graphic artist Harvey Kurtzman. Neil Gaiman was inspired by a collection of this name to write a collection of stories set in a graveyard. A "second" collection of this name contains a story in which a village is destroyed after (*) Messua and her husband are imprisoned for adopting a child. In a 1894 collection of this name, Little Toomai returns to camp after seeing elephants dance and two snakes are killed by a mongoose. For 10 points, give this title of a Rudyard Kipling collection featuring Rikki-Tikki-Tavi and Mowgli.
tossups.question_sanitized: Karim is asked by Shadwell to wear a loincloth and use an accent while performing in a play of this name in The Buddha of Suburbia. A collection of adult satirical stories with this name was written by graphic artist Harvey Kurtzman. Neil Gaiman was inspired by a collection of this name to write a collection of stories set in a graveyard. A "second" collection of this name contains a story in which a village is destroyed after (*) Messua and her husband are imprisoned for adopting a child. In a 1894 collection of this name, Little Toomai returns to camp after seeing elephants dance and two snakes are killed by a mongoose. For 10 points, give this title of a Rudyard Kipling collection featuring Rikki-Tikki-Tavi and Mowgli.

tossups.question: One story in this collection concerns Kotick, a white seal who finds a safe place to lead his brethren away from fur hunters. Nag and Nagaina [nag-ah-ee-nah] are cobras who conspire to kill one title character of a story in this book; that is the mongoose Rikki Tikki Tavi. The protagonist of the best-known stories from this book is kidnapped by Bandar Log monkeys, but is rescued by Bagheera and Kaa, a panther and serpent. That protagonist is a human boy raised by the wolf Akela. For 10 points, Mowgli and Baloo the bear appear in what collection by Rudyard Kipling?
tossups.question_sanitized: One story in this collection concerns Kotick, a white seal who finds a safe place to lead his brethren away from fur hunters. Nag and Nagaina [nag-ah-ee-nah] are cobras who conspire to kill one title character of a story in this book; that is the mongoose Rikki Tikki Tavi. The protagonist of the best-known stories from this book is kidnapped by Bandar Log monkeys, but is rescued by Bagheera and Kaa, a panther and serpent. That protagonist is a human boy raised by the wolf Akela. For 10 points, Mowgli and Baloo the bear appear in what collection by Rudyard Kipling?

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Russian composer Sofia Gubaidulina (“goo-bye-DOO-lin-uh”), who died in 2025. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about Russian composer Sofia Gubaidulina ("goo-bye-DOO-lin-uh"), who died in 2025. For 10 points each:
parts: Gubaidulina’s 1980 piece Offertorium was a piece in this genre dedicated to Gidon Kremer. Hilary Hahn recorded an E minor piece in this genre by Felix Mendelssohn with the Oslo Philharmonic in 2002.
Gubaidulina also wrote the score for the 1967 Soviet animated film Adventures of Mowgli, which was based on this Rudyard Kipling collection whose Disney adaptation was released the same year.

Gubaidulina dedicated her piece Canticle of the Sun to this Russian cellist, dubbed by Julian Lloyd Webber as “probably the greatest cellist of all time.” This cellist was the dedicatee of both of Shostakovich’s cello concerti and Prokofiev's cello sonata.

Gubaidulina also wrote the score for the 1967 Soviet animated film Adventures of Mowgli, which was based on this Rudyard Kipling collection whose Disney adaptation was released the same year.

bonuses.leadin: Rómulo Gallegos’ novel Canaima is in a subgenre of novela de la tierra set in this kind of place, which often featured idealistic protagonists overwhelmed by the forces of nature. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Romulo Gallegos' novel Canaima is in a subgenre of novela de la tierra set in this kind of place, which often featured idealistic protagonists overwhelmed by the forces of nature. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this kind of natural setting that also titles a set of Kipling stories about the “man-cub” Mowgli.

An unstable poet is “devoured” by the jungle in this country’s novel The Vortex. A character in a novel from this country named Rebeca starts a plague of insomnia and carries around a bag of her parents' bones.

A father refuses to accept his boy’s death while hunting in “The Son,” one of this author’s many stories set in the jungle of Misiones. In a story by this author, four brothers behead their sister after seeing how dinner is prepared.
parts_sanitized: Name this kind of natural setting that also titles a set of Kipling stories about the "man-cub" Mowgli.

An unstable poet is "devoured" by the jungle in this country's novel The Vortex. A character in a novel from this country named Rebeca starts a plague of insomnia and carries around a bag of her parents' bones.

A father refuses to accept his boy's death while hunting in "The Son," one of this author's many stories set in the jungle of Misiones. In a story by this author, four brothers behead their sister after seeing how dinner is prepared.
answers: the jungle [accept the rainforest; accept la novela de la selva; accept The Jungle Book; prompt on the wilderness]
Republic of Colombia (The second novel is One Hundred Years of Solitude.)
Horacio Quiroga [or Horacio Silvestre Quiroga Forteza] (The second story is “The Decapitated Chicken.”)
answers_sanitized: the jungle [accept the rainforest; accept la novela de la selva; accept The Jungle Book; prompt on the wilderness]

bonuses.leadin: This novel begins with the playwright Amma opening her show The Last Amazon of Dahomey in London. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel by Bernardine Evaristo about the intersecting experiences of twelve women, which ends with characters reuniting at the National Theatre.
In Hanif Kureishi's The Buddha of Suburbia, Karim joins the London theater scene and portrays the "man-cub" Mowgli in a stage adaptation of this Rudyard Kipling collection.

Sarah Waters draws on this literary genre in her novel Tipping the Velvet, in which Nan runs away to London and becomes a cross-dressing theater entertainer. Novels in this genre, which typically depict a roguish lower-class hero's adventures, include Tom Jones and Moll Flanders.

bonuses.leadin: An author with this surname published the Modernist poetry collection The Hallucinated City and created a shapeshifting trickster of the Tapanhumas tribe. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this surname of Mário, the author of Macunaíma (ma-koo-na-EE-muh). Another Group of Five member with this surname wrote "Tupi or not Tupi" in the Anthropophagic Manifesto.

Macunaíma opens with the title character's birth in a "virgin" one of these places. A Horacio Quiroga (oh-RAHSS-yo kee-ROH-ga) book named for these places was inspired by a similarly titled Kipling book about Mowgli's life in one of these places.
The "Letter to the Icamiabas" section of Macunaíma was inspired by the first-person narrated Cantos III and IV of this epic poem about the voyages of Vasco da Gama.
answers: de Andrade (jee on-DRAH-jee) [accept Mário de Andrade or Oswald de Andrade]
answers_sanitized: de Andrade (jee on-DRAH-jee) [accept Mario de Andrade or Oswald de Andrade]
jungles [accept selvas; accept Jungle Tales or The Jungle Book]

bonuses.leadin: This term names a type of cultural narrative which is called a pourquoi ("poor-KWAH") tale in French. For 10 points each:
parts: Give the two-word term for fantastic origin tales. Several of them which a British author told to his daughter Effie, such as "How the Whale Got His Throat," title a 1902 book.

bonuses.leadin: This play ends with a monologue in which a character says that he and another woman will “be together in our bear’s cave, and our squirrel’s drey, and we’ll live on honey, and nuts.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this play in which Helena leaves Jimmy Porter’s residence after Alison returns and tells her that she lost the baby she was carrying after going home with her father, Colonel Redfern.

parts: What author created the title character Kimball O’Hara in the novel Kim, as well as the mongoose Rikki Tikki Tavi?

bonuses.leadin: 12-year-old Yukiko runs away with 17-year-old Mitsuo in one novel by this author, and the two relive events from the first couple of years after WWII, including dying in a shipwreck, of cholera, and in a dog attack. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this daughter of Dazai Osamu and author of Laughing Wolf, who wrote several short stories about women and children often abandoned by a husband, parent, or someone else in the collection The Shooting Gallery.

Throughout Laughing Wolf, Mitsuo sings songs from this book to himself and tries to instill in Yukiko a respect for “the Law”. Mitsuo and Yukiko also take the names Akela and Mowgli from characters in this Kipling novel.

One of Tsushima’s best known stories is named for this method of goods exchange by parties incapable of communicating with one another. This practice is most associated with gold-for-salt trade in West Africa.
answers: "Silent Trade" [or Silent Barter or Dumb Barter or Depot Trade]
